#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed on RHEL 8
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    aide --init
    mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
fi

# Ensure AIDE is enabled and running at boot
systemctl enable aidecheck.service
systemctl start aidecheck.service

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed on RHEL 8
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    aide --init
    mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
fi

# Ensure AIDE is enabled and running at boot
systemctl enable aidecheck.service
systemctl start aidecheck.service

#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit tools to be monitored by AIDE
audit_tools=(
    /usr/bin/ausearch
    /usr/sbin/aureport
    /sbin/auditctl
    /sbin/audispd
    /sbin/auditd
)

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration file
if [ ! -f /etc/aide/aide.conf.bak ]; then
    cp /etc/aide/aide.conf /etc/aide/aide.conf.bak
fi

# Function to add or update entries in AIDE configuration
update_aide_config() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    if grep -q "^$tool_path" /etc/aide/aide.conf; then
        sed -i "s|^$tool_path.*|$tool_path = R, p, g, m, a|g" /etc/aide/aide.conf
    else
        echo "$tool_path = R, p, g, m, a" >> /etc/aide/aide.conf
    fi
}

# Register each audit tool with AIDE for monitoring
for tool in "${audit_tools[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$tool" ]; then
        update_aide_config "$tool"
    else
        echo "Warning: $tool does not exist and will not be monitored by AIDE."
    fi
done

# Update the AIDE database to include the new or updated entries
aide --init
mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz

# Ensure AIDE service is enabled and started
systemctl enable aide
systemctl start aide

echo "AIDE has been configured to monitor audit tools."

    echo "GRUB configuration does not include fips=1"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure GnuTLS honors system-wide cryptographic policy
# This script sets up GnuTLS to use the system's crypto policy
# and disables legacy/insecure protocol versions.

# Define the path to the GnuTLS configuration file
GNUTLS_CONF="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/gnutls.config"

# Check if the GnuTLS configuration file exists
if [ ! -f "$GNUTLS_CONF" ]; then
  echo "GnuTLS configuration file not found at $GNUTLS_CONF"
  exit 1
fi

# Define the required settings for the GnuTLS configuration
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
  "DEFAULT_PRIORITY = \"SYSTEM\""
)

# Function to check if a setting is already present in the configuration file
setting_exists() {
  local setting="$1"
  grep -q "^$setting" "$GNUTLS_CONF"
}

# Apply the required settings
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
  if ! setting_exists "$setting"; then
    echo "Adding $setting to $GNUTLS_CONF"
    echo "$setting" >> "$GNUTLS_CONF"
  else
    echo "$setting already exists in $GNUTLS_CONF"
  fi
done

# Ensure the GnuTLS configuration is reloaded
echo "Reloading GnuTLS configuration..."
update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT

# Verify the settings have been applied correctly
if setting_exists "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[0]}"; then
  echo "GnuTLS configuration has been successfully updated to comply with system-wide cryptographic policy."
else
  echo "Failed to update GnuTLS configuration. Please check $GNUTLS_CONF for errors."
  exit 1
fi

  echo "GnuTLS configuration file not found at $GNUTLS_CONF"
  exit 1
fi

# Define the required settings for the GnuTLS configuration
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
  "DEFAULT_PRIORITY = \"SYSTEM\""
)

# Function to check if a setting is already present in the configuration file
setting_exists() {
  local setting="$1"
  grep -q "^$setting" "$GNUTLS_CONF"
}

# Apply the required settings
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
  if ! setting_exists "$setting"; then
    echo "Adding $setting to $GNUTLS_CONF"
    echo "$setting" >> "$GNUTLS_CONF"
  else
    echo "$setting already exists in $GNUTLS_CONF"
  fi
done

# Ensure the GnuTLS configuration is reloaded
echo "Reloading GnuTLS configuration..."
update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT

# Verify the settings have been applied correctly
if setting_exists "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[0]}"; then
  echo "GnuTLS configuration has been successfully updated to comply with system-wide cryptographic policy."
else
  echo "Failed to update GnuTLS configuration. Please check $GNUTLS_CONF for errors."
  exit 1
fi
```

SUDO_TIMEOUT=15

# Ensure the /etc/sudoers file is backed up
sudo cp -a /etc/sudoers /etc/sudoers.bak

# Use visudo to safely edit the sudoers file
sudo visudo -c <<EOF
Defaults env_reset
Defaults mail_badpass
Defaults secure_path="/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
Defaults timestamp_timeout=${SUDO_TIMEOUT}
EOF

# Check if the edit was successful
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed to update sudoers file. Restoring backup..."
    sudo cp -a /etc/sudoers.bak /etc/sudoers
    exit 1
fi

# Remove the backup if everything is fine
sudo rm -f /etc/sudoers.bak

# Check if the RPM configuration file exists

    echo "Error: /etc/rpm/macros file does not exist."
    exit 1

if grep -q "^%_gpgcheck" /etc/rpm/macros; then

    echo "$REQUIRED_MACRO" >> /etc/rpm/macros
fi

# Verify that the change has been applied correctly

    echo "GPG signature verification for local package installations is enabled."
else
    echo "Failed to enable GPG signature verification for local package installations."

    echo "Banner text set and locked successfully."

# Ensure PAM is configured to lock root account on failed login attempts

# Backup original files if they haven't been backed up yet
if [ ! -f /etc/pam.d/system-auth.bak ]; then
    cp /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/system-auth.bak
fi

if [ ! -f /etc/pam.d/password-auth.bak ]; then
    cp /etc/pam.d/password-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth.bak
fi

# Ensure the required PAM modules are present and configured correctly
sed -i '/^auth.*required.*pam_tally2.so.*/d' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
sed -i '/^account.*required.*pam_tally2.so.*/d' /etc/pam.d/system-auth

sed -i '/^auth.*required.*pam_tally2.so.*/d' /etc/pam.d/password-auth
sed -i '/^account.*required.*pam_tally2.so.*/d' /etc/pam.d/password-auth

# Add the necessary lines to enforce lockout policy
echo "auth required pam_tally2.so onerr=fail deny=5 unlock_time=600" | tee -a /etc/pam.d/system-auth > /dev/null
echo "account required pam_tally2.so per_user" | tee -a /etc/pam.d/system-auth > /dev/null

echo "account required pam_tally2.so per_user" | tee -a /etc/pam.d/password-auth > /dev/null

# Ensure the lockout settings are applied to root

#!/bin/bash

# Define the desired permission mode
desired_mode="0740"

# Function to apply permissions recursively to user's dot-files
apply_permissions() {
    local home_dir="$1"
    # Find all dot-files and directories in the home directory
    find "$home_dir" -maxdepth 1 -type d,f -name ".*" -exec chmod "$desired_mode" {} +
}

# Apply permissions for root user
apply_permissions "/root"

# Iterate over all users with a home directory
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid gid comment home shell; do
    # Skip system accounts (UID < 1000) and non-interactive shells
    if (( uid >= 1000 )) && [[ "$shell" != /sbin/nologin ]] && [[ "$shell" != /bin/false ]]; then
        apply_permissions "$home"
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

    local home_dir="$1"
    # Find all dot-files and directories in the home directory
    find "$home_dir" -maxdepth 1 -type d,f -name ".*" -exec chmod "$desired_mode" {} +
}

while IFS=: read -r username _ uid gid comment home shell; do

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Please run this script as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to enable Kernel Page-Table Isolation (KPTI) for a given kernel version
enable_kpti_for_kernel() {
    local kernel_version="$1"
    local grub_cfg="/etc/grub2-efi.cfg"
    
    # Check if the kernel version is already configured with KPTI
    if grep -q "linux.*$kernel_version.*spectre_v2=on" "$grub_cfg"; then
        echo "KPTI is already enabled for kernel $kernel_version"
        return
    fi

    # Update GRUB configuration to enable KPTI for the specified kernel version
    sed -i "/linux.*$kernel_version/s/$/ spectre_v2=on/" "$grub_cfg"
    echo "Enabled KPTI for kernel $kernel_version"
}

# Get all installed kernel versions
installed_kernels=$(rpm -q --last kernel | awk '{print $1}' | sort -V)

# Enable KPTI for each installed kernel version
for kernel in $installed_kernels; do
    enable_kpti_for_kernel "$kernel"
done

# Update GRUB to apply changes
grub2-mkconfig -o /etc/grub2-efi.cfg

# Ensure that any newly installed kernels will also have KPTI enabled
echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"\$GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX spectre_v2=on\"" >> /etc/default/grub

# Update GRUB again to apply the new default settings
grub2-mkconfig -o /etc/grub2-efi.cfg

echo "KPTI has been enabled for all installed kernels and will persist across reboots."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Please run this script as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to update GRUB configuration for a specific kernel version
update_grub_for_kernel() {
    local kernel_version="$1"
    local grub_config="/etc/default/grub.d/99-disable-legacy-vsyscall.conf"

    # Create or update the GRUB configuration file if it doesn't exist
    if ! grep -q "vsyscall=none" "$grub_config"; then
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"\${GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX} vsyscall=none\"" > "$grub_config"
        grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
    fi
}

# Get all installed kernel versions
kernel_versions=$(rpm -q --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel)

# Update GRUB configuration for each installed kernel version
for kernel_version in $kernel_versions; do
    update_grub_for_kernel "$kernel_version"

    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
fi

echo "Legacy virtual syscall capability has been disabled for all installed kernels and future kernels."

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [ "$EUID" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Please run this script as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to update GRUB configuration for a specific kernel version
update_grub_for_kernel() {
    local kernel_version="$1"
    local grub_config="/etc/default/grub.d/99-disable-legacy-vsyscall.conf"

    # Create or update the GRUB configuration file if it doesn't exist
    if ! grep -q "vsyscall=none" "$grub_config"; then
        echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"\${GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX} vsyscall=none\"" > "$grub_config"
        grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
    fi
}

# Get all installed kernel versions
kernel_versions=$(rpm -q --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel)

# Update GRUB configuration for each installed kernel version
for kernel_version in $kernel_versions; do
    update_grub_for_kernel "$kernel_version"
done

# Ensure the setting is applied to future kernels by updating the default GRUB configuration
default_grub="/etc/default/grub"
if ! grep -q "vsyscall=none" "$default_grub"; then
    sed -i 's/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="\(.*\)"/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="\1 vsyscall=none"/' "$default_grub"
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
fi

echo "Legacy virtual syscall capability has been disabled for all installed kernels and future kernels."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y rsyslog
fi

# Enable and start rsyslog service
systemctl enable --now rsyslog

# Configure TLS for log forwarding
TLS_CERT="/etc/rsyslog.d/tls.crt"
TLS_KEY="/etc/rsyslog.d/tls.key"
REMOTE_COLLECTOR="remote-collector.example.com"

# Create a directory for TLS certificates if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/rsyslog.d

# Generate self-signed certificate and key if they don't exist
if [[ ! -f "$TLS_CERT" || ! -f "$TLS_KEY" ]]; then
    openssl req -x509 -nodes -days 365 -newkey rsa:2048 \
        -keyout "$TLS_KEY" -out "$TLS_CERT" \
        -subj "/C=US/ST=State/L=City/O=Organization/CN=$REMOTE_COLLECTOR"
fi

# Configure rsyslog to use TLS
cat <<EOF > /etc/rsyslog.d/remote-logging.conf
\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls
\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode anon
\$ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer *
\$ActionSendStreamDriverCAFile $TLS_CERT
*.* @@$REMOTE_COLLECTOR:514?RSYSLOG_TcpTransport=on&SyslogProtocol23Format=yes
EOF

# Reload rsyslog to apply changes
systemctl reload rsyslog

#!/bin/bash

# Function to check if a filesystem is mounted
is_mounted() {
    mount | grep -q " on $1 "
}

# Check if /boot is a separate partition
if df --output=source,target /boot | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}' | grep -q '^/dev'; then
    BOOT_PARTITION=$(df --output=source,target /boot | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}')
else
    echo "Boot partition is not a separate filesystem. No action needed."
    exit 0
fi

# Check if the noexec option is already set for the boot partition
if mount | grep -q "$BOOT_PARTITION.*noexec"; then
    echo "Noexec option is already set for the boot partition."
else
    # Add noexec option to /etc/fstab
    sed -i "/$BOOT_PARTITION/ s/defaults/defaults,noexec/" /etc/fstab

    # Remount the boot partition with the new options
    mount -o remount,noexec $BOOT_PARTITION

    echo "Noexec option added and applied to the boot partition."
fi

# Check if nosuid option is already set for the boot partition
if mount | grep -q "$BOOT_PARTITION.*nosuid"; then
    echo "Nosuid option is already set for the boot partition."
else
    # Add nosuid option to /etc/fstab
    sed -i "/$BOOT_PARTITION/ s/defaults/defaults,nosuid/" /etc/fstab

    # Remount the boot partition with the new options
    mount -o remount,nosuid $BOOT_PARTITION

    echo "Nosuid option added and applied to the boot partition."
fi

# Check if nodev option is already set for the boot partition
if mount | grep -q "$BOOT_PARTITION.*nodev"; then
    echo "Nodev option is already set for the boot partition."
else
    # Add nodev option to /etc/fstab
    sed -i "/$BOOT_PARTITION/ s/defaults/defaults,nodev/" /etc/fstab

    # Remount the boot partition with the new options
    mount -o remount,nodev $BOOT_PARTITION

    echo "Nodev option added and applied to the boot partition."
fi

echo "Remediation complete for boot partition security settings."

# Ensure /dev/shm has noexec,nosuid,nodev options
mount | grep -q 'on /dev/shm type tmpfs' && {
    current_options=$(mount | grep 'on /dev/shm type tmpfs' | awk '{print $6}')
    if [[ ! "$current_options" =~ "noexec" || ! "$current_options" =~ "nosuid" || ! "$current_options" =~ "nodev" ]]; then

        umount /dev/shm

    fi
}

    echo "tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs noexec,nosuid,nodev 0 0" >> /etc/fstab
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Define the root filesystem mount point
ROOT_MOUNT="/"

# Get a list of all non-root local filesystems
NON_ROOT_FILESYSTEMS=$(findmnt -t ext4,xfs,btrfs -o TARGET -n | grep -v "^$ROOT_MOUNT")

# Iterate over each non-root filesystem and apply the necessary mount options
for FS in $NON_ROOT_FILESYSTEMS; do
    # Check if the filesystem is already mounted with the correct options
    if ! findmnt -t ext4,xfs,btrfs -o OPTIONS -n "$FS" | grep -q "nodev"; then
        # If not, remount it with the nodev option
        mount -o remount,nodev "$FS"

else
    # Append the configuration to /etc/sysctl.conf

        echo "Failed to append sysctl parameter $SYSCTL_PARAM to /etc/sysctl.conf."

    fi
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Define the kernel parameter to enable object poisoning
KPARAM="slab_poison=1"

# Function to add a kernel parameter to GRUB configuration
add_kernel_param() {
    local param="$1"
    local grub_cfg="/etc/default/grub"

    # Check if the parameter is already set
    if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\".*$param.*\"" "$grub_cfg"; then
        echo "Parameter $param is already set in GRUB configuration."
        return 0
    fi

    # Add the parameter to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
    sed -i.bak "s/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"/&$param /" "$grub_cfg"
    echo "Added $param to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX."

    # Update GRUB configuration
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
    echo "Updated GRUB configuration."
}

# Apply the kernel parameter for all currently installed kernels
for kver in $(rpm -qa --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel); do
    if [ ! -f "/etc/default/grub-$kver" ]; then
        cp /etc/default/grub "/etc/default/grub-$kver"
    fi

    # Add the parameter to each kernel's GRUB configuration
    add_kernel_param "$KPARAM" < "/etc/default/grub-$kver"
done

# Ensure the parameter is applied for future kernels
add_kernel_param "$KPARAM"

echo "Kernel memory allocator object-poisoning feature enabled."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure SELinux is enforcing to enable application whitelisting
if ! sestatus | grep -q "Current mode: enforcing"; then
    setenforce 1
fi

# Install the necessary package for file access policy daemon
if ! rpm -q selinux-policy-devel &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y selinux-policy-devel
fi

# Ensure the SELinux targeted policy is installed and active
if ! rpm -q selinux-policy-targeted &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y selinux-policy-targeted
fi

# Set SELinux to targeted mode if not already set
if ! sestatus | grep -q "Policy from config file: targeted"; then
    sed -i 's/^SELINUX=.*/SELINUX=targeted/' /etc/selinux/config
fi

# Ensure the SELinux policy is relabeled if necessary
if [ "$(getenforce)" == "Enforcing" ]; then
    touch /.autorelabel
fi
```

    local updated_grub=false

    # Check if the GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line exists and contains the desired setting
    if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$grub_cfg"; then
        # Update the existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line with the audit backlog limit

#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit rule
AUDIT_RULE='-w /var/log/lastlog -p wa -k logins'

# Function to add an audit rule using augenrules
add_audit_rule_augenrules() {
    if ! grep -q "^$AUDIT_RULE$" /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; then
        echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/99-logins.rules
        augenrules --load
    fi
}

# Function to add an audit rule using auditctl
add_audit_rule_auditctl() {
    if ! auditctl -l | grep -q "^$AUDIT_RULE$"; then
        echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> /etc/audit/rules.d/99-logins.rules
        auditctl -R /etc/audit/rules.d/99-logins.rules
    fi
}

# Ensure the directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Add the audit rule using augenrules or auditctl, whichever is available
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    add_audit_rule_augenrules
elif command -v auditctl &> /dev/null; then
    add_audit_rule_auditctl
fi

[Unit]
Description=Reload audit rules on boot

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/sbin/augenrules --load

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

systemctl daemon-reload
